judgments.comparative_verdict: Both are near-identical and correct on a, b, and d; Report 2 answers slightly better because it explicitly endorses the going-forward practice of updating docs in the same commit as the code, which Report 1 omits.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should go in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... An exhaustive grep for `chargekit` ... shows exactly two violating call sites: - `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... - `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` ... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` also imports from `chargekit`, but only the error classes ... which both the doc and that file's own docstring explicitly carve out as the permitted exception.

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... found exactly two violations: - `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4` and `:14` ... - `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2` and `:9` ... The only other SDK references are the gateway itself, the sanctioned `errors.py` exception, and a prose mention in `README.md:4`.

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer, `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:15-22`, has a sixth field, `external_ref` ... migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` added the `external_ref` column

report_2.evidence: The actual response model `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref` ... Migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` shows this column was added

before or at the very start of the refund work, as its own small commit — not bundled silently into the refund feature, and definitely not left for "later." ... Thereafter, any refund commit that changes the API shape, the boundary, or the webhook set must update the doc in the same commit

report_1.evidence: drop the hard-coded count ("handles 4 webhook event types") and phrase it as "handles the following event types: …" or point to `app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth. An exact count is the most brittle possible phrasing — it silently broke the moment `payment_pending` was added, and it will break again

report_2.evidence: drop the hardcoded count entirely. A literal number like "4" is guaranteed to drift every time a handler branch is added — as it already has ... Restate it as a property plus a pointer to the single source of truth
